title: Examples
text: simple bar chart compares values across categories. bar represents one category height proportional value. dodged bar chart places bars sub-group side side, making easy compare values within across categories. stacked bar chart layers sub-groups top within category, showing individual contributions total.
code: bar_data <- data.frame(   Product = c("Laptop", "Tablet", "Phone", "Monitor"),   Sales = c(150, 230, 180, 290) )  p <- ggplot(bar_data, aes(x = Product, y = Sales)) +   geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "steelblue") +   labs(     title = "Product Sales by Category",     x = "Product",     y = "Sales (units)"   ) +   theme_minimal()  p products <- c("Laptop", "Tablet", "Phone", "Monitor") sales <- c(150, 230, 180, 290)  barplot(sales,   names.arg = products,   col = "steelblue",   main = "Product Sales by Category",   xlab = "Product",   ylab = "Sales (units)" ) dodged_data <- data.frame(   Region = rep(c("North", "South", "East"), each = 2),   Quarter = rep(c("Q1", "Q2"), 3),   Revenue = c(120, 150, 200, 180, 160, 210) )  p <- ggplot(dodged_data, aes(x = Region, y = Revenue, fill = Quarter)) +   geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.8)) +   labs(title = "Quarterly Revenue by Region") +   scale_fill_manual(values = c("steelblue", "coral")) +   theme_minimal()  p revenue_matrix <- matrix(c(120, 150, 200, 180, 160, 210), nrow = 2) rownames(revenue_matrix) <- c("Q1", "Q2") barplot(revenue_matrix,   beside = TRUE,   names.arg = c("North", "South", "East"),   col = c("steelblue", "coral"),   legend.text = rownames(revenue_matrix),   main = "Quarterly Revenue by Region",   xlab = "Region",   ylab = "Revenue" ) stacked_data <- data.frame(   Year = rep(c("2022", "2023", "2024"), each = 3),   Source = rep(c("Solar", "Wind", "Hydro"), 3),   Output = c(40, 30, 50, 55, 45, 48, 70, 60, 52) )  p <- ggplot(stacked_data, aes(x = Year, y = Output, fill = Source)) +   geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_stack()) +   labs(     title = "Renewable Energy Output by Source",     y = "Output (GWh)"   ) +   scale_fill_manual(values = c("#2ecc71", "#3498db", "#9b59b6")) +   theme_minimal()  p energy_matrix <- matrix(   c(40, 30, 50, 55, 45, 48, 70, 60, 52),   nrow = 3 ) rownames(energy_matrix) <- c("Solar", "Wind", "Hydro") barplot(energy_matrix,   beside = FALSE,   names.arg = c("2022", "2023", "2024"),   col = c("#2ecc71", "#3498db", "#9b59b6"),   legend.text = rownames(energy_matrix),   main = "Renewable Energy Output by Source",   xlab = "Year",   ylab = "Output (GWh)" )

title: Examples
text: histogram shows frequency distribution continuous variable grouping values bins. height bar indicates many observations fall within range.
code: exam_scores <- data.frame(score = c(   rnorm(500, mean = 72, sd = 10),   rnorm(500, mean = 85, sd = 8) ))  p <- ggplot(exam_scores, aes(x = score)) +   geom_histogram(bins = 25, fill = "skyblue", color = "white") +   labs(     title = "Distribution of Exam Scores",     x = "Score",     y = "Frequency"   ) +   theme_minimal()  p scores <- c(rnorm(500, mean = 72, sd = 10), rnorm(500, mean = 85, sd = 8)) hist(scores,   breaks = 25,   col = "skyblue",   border = "white",   main = "Distribution of Exam Scores",   xlab = "Score",   ylab = "Frequency" )

title: Examples
text: scatter plot displays relationship two continuous variables. point represents one observation, color can encode third categorical variable.
code: car_data <- data.frame(   weight = mtcars$wt,   mpg = mtcars$mpg,   cylinders = factor(mtcars$cyl) )  p <- ggplot(car_data, aes(x = weight, y = mpg, color = cylinders)) +   geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.8) +   labs(     title = "Fuel Efficiency vs Vehicle Weight",     x = "Weight (1000 lbs)",     y = "Miles per Gallon",     color = "Cylinders"   ) +   theme_minimal()  p colors <- c("4" = "steelblue", "6" = "coral", "8" = "forestgreen") plot(mtcars$wt, mtcars$mpg,   pch = 19,   col = colors[as.character(mtcars$cyl)],   main = "Fuel Efficiency vs Vehicle Weight",   xlab = "Weight (1000 lbs)",   ylab = "Miles per Gallon" ) legend("topright",   legend = c("4 cyl", "6 cyl", "8 cyl"),   col = colors, pch = 19 )

text: line plot connects ordered data points, making ideal showing trends time sequential categories. multi-line plot overlays several series axes, enabling direct comparison trends across groups.
code: temp_data <- data.frame(   Month = factor(month.abb, levels = month.abb),   Temperature = c(2, 4, 10, 15, 20, 25, 28, 27, 22, 15, 8, 3) )  p <- ggplot(temp_data, aes(x = Month, y = Temperature, group = 1)) +   geom_line(color = "tomato", linewidth = 1.2) +   labs(     title = "Average Monthly Temperature",     y = "Temperature (C)"   ) +   theme_minimal()  p months <- 1:12 temps <- c(2, 4, 10, 15, 20, 25, 28, 27, 22, 15, 8, 3) plot(months, temps,   type = "l", col = "tomato", lwd = 2,   main = "Average Monthly Temperature",   xlab = "Month", ylab = "Temperature (C)",   xaxt = "n" ) axis(1, at = 1:12, labels = month.abb) multi_line <- data.frame(   Year = rep(2015:2024, 3),   Users = c(     10, 15, 22, 35, 50, 72, 95, 120, 150, 180,     8, 12, 18, 25, 38, 55, 70, 88, 110, 135,     5, 8, 14, 20, 30, 42, 58, 75, 95, 118   ),   Platform = rep(c("Mobile", "Desktop", "Tablet"), each = 10) )  p <- ggplot(multi_line, aes(x = Year, y = Users, color = Platform)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) +   labs(     title = "Platform Users Over Time",     y = "Users (millions)"   ) +   theme_minimal()  p years <- 2015:2024 users <- cbind(   Mobile  = c(10, 15, 22, 35, 50, 72, 95, 120, 150, 180),   Desktop = c(8, 12, 18, 25, 38, 55, 70, 88, 110, 135),   Tablet  = c(5, 8, 14, 20, 30, 42, 58, 75, 95, 118) )  matplot(years, users,   type = "l", lwd = 2,   col = c("steelblue", "coral", "forestgreen"),   lty = 1,   main = "Platform Users Over Time",   xlab = "Year", ylab = "Users (millions)" ) legend("topleft",   legend = colnames(users),   col = c("steelblue", "coral", "forestgreen"),   lwd = 2 )

text: violin plot combines kernel density estimation (KDE) curves box-summary statistics, providing richer view data distribution box plot alone. MAIDR renders violin two navigable layers: box layer (min, Q1, median, Q3, max) KDE layer (density curve). Note: Violin plots ggplot2-feature.

title: Examples
text: heatmap uses color intensity represent values two-dimensional matrix. useful spotting patterns, clusters, outliers across two categorical dimensions.
code: heatmap_data <- expand.grid(   Day = c("Mon", "Tue", "Wed", "Thu", "Fri"),   Hour = c("9am", "10am", "11am", "12pm", "1pm", "2pm", "3pm", "4pm") ) heatmap_data$Visitors <- c(   20, 35, 50, 45, 30,   25, 40, 60, 55, 35,   30, 55, 75, 70, 45,   40, 65, 90, 85, 60,   35, 50, 70, 65, 40,   30, 45, 60, 55, 38,   25, 40, 55, 50, 32,   15, 30, 40, 35, 22 )  p <- ggplot(heatmap_data, aes(x = Day, y = Hour, fill = Visitors)) +   geom_tile(color = "white") +   scale_fill_gradient(low = "#f7fbff", high = "#08306b") +   labs(title = "Website Visitors by Day and Hour") +   theme_minimal()  p visitors <- matrix(   c(     20, 35, 50, 45, 30,     25, 40, 60, 55, 35,     30, 55, 75, 70, 45,     40, 65, 90, 85, 60,     35, 50, 70, 65, 40,     30, 45, 60, 55, 38,     25, 40, 55, 50, 32,     15, 30, 40, 35, 22   ),   nrow = 5 ) image(visitors,   col = hcl.colors(20, "Blues"),   main = "Website Visitors by Day and Hour",   xlab = "Day",   ylab = "Hour",   axes = FALSE ) axis(1,   at = seq(0, 1, length.out = 5),   labels = c("Mon", "Tue", "Wed", "Thu", "Fri") ) axis(2,   at = seq(0, 1, length.out = 8),   labels = c("9am", "10am", "11am", "12pm", "1pm", "2pm", "3pm", "4pm") )

code: p <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(x = mpg)) +   geom_histogram(     aes(y = after_stat(density)),     bins = 15, fill = "lightblue", color = "white"   ) +   geom_density(color = "red", linewidth = 1.2) +   labs(title = "MPG: Histogram with Density Curve") +   theme_minimal()  p hist(mtcars$mpg,   breaks = 15, freq = FALSE,   col = "lightblue", border = "white",   main = "MPG: Histogram with Density Curve",   xlab = "Miles per Gallon",   ylab = "Density" ) lines(density(mtcars$mpg), col = "red", lwd = 2) combo_data <- data.frame(   month = factor(month.abb[1:6], levels = month.abb[1:6]),   sales = c(100, 120, 90, 150, 130, 160),   target = c(110, 110, 110, 140, 140, 140) )  p <- ggplot(combo_data, aes(x = month)) +   geom_bar(aes(y = sales), stat = "identity", fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.7) +   geom_line(aes(y = target, group = 1), color = "red", linewidth = 1.5) +   labs(title = "Monthly Sales vs Target", y = "Value") +   theme_minimal()  p

title: Examples
text: Multi-panel layouts arrange several independent plots grid. useful dashboards comparing different views dataset.
code: library(patchwork)  # Line plot with currency formatting line_df <- data.frame(   Month = 1:8,   Revenue = c(2500, 4200, 3100, 5500, 4300, 6700, 5600, 7800) ) pw_line <- ggplot(line_df, aes(Month, Revenue)) +   geom_line(color = "steelblue", linewidth = 1) +   labs(title = "Monthly Revenue", x = "Month", y = "Revenue") +   theme_minimal()  # Bar plot with rates bar_df1 <- data.frame(   Category = c("A", "B", "C", "D", "E"),   Rate = c(0.15, 0.22, 0.18, 0.28, 0.17) ) pw_bar1 <- ggplot(bar_df1, aes(Category, Rate)) +   geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "forestgreen", alpha = 0.7) +   labs(title = "Conversion Rates", x = "Category", y = "Rate") +   theme_minimal()  # Bar plot with large numbers bar_df2 <- data.frame(   Category = c("A", "B", "C", "D", "E"),   Count = c(125000, 98000, 145000, 112000, 88000) ) pw_bar2 <- ggplot(bar_df2, aes(Category, Count)) +   geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "royalblue", alpha = 0.7) +   labs(title = "User Counts", x = "Category", y = "Count") +   theme_minimal()  # Line plot with exponential growth line_df2 <- data.frame(   x = 1:8,   y = 10^(seq(3, 6.5, length.out = 8)) ) pw_line2 <- ggplot(line_df2, aes(x, y)) +   geom_line(color = "tomato", linewidth = 1) +   labs(title = "Exponential Growth", x = "Time", y = "Value") +   theme_minimal()  combined <- (pw_line + pw_bar1 + pw_bar2 + pw_line2) +   plot_layout(ncol = 2) combined par(mfrow = c(2, 2))  barplot(table(mtcars$cyl),   col = "steelblue",   main = "Cars by Cylinder Count",   xlab = "Cylinders" )  hist(mtcars$mpg,   breaks = 12, col = "coral", border = "white",   main = "MPG Distribution", xlab = "MPG" )  plot(mtcars$wt, mtcars$mpg,   pch = 19, col = "forestgreen",   main = "MPG vs Weight",   xlab = "Weight (1000 lbs)", ylab = "MPG" )  boxplot(hp ~ gear,   data = mtcars, col = "plum",   main = "Horsepower by Gear Count",   xlab = "Gears", ylab = "Horsepower" ) invisible(par(mfrow = c(1, 1)))

title: Examples
text: Faceted plots split data grid subplots one grouping variables. panel shows type chart different subset data. ggplot2-feature using facet_wrap() facet_grid().
code: facet_data <- data.frame(   x = rep(c("A", "B", "C", "D"), 4),   y = c(     30, 25, 35, 20,     45, 30, 25, 40,     20, 35, 30, 45,     35, 40, 20, 30   ),   panel = rep(c("Group 1", "Group 2", "Group 3", "Group 4"), each = 4) )  p <- ggplot(facet_data, aes(x = x, y = y)) +   geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "steelblue") +   facet_wrap(~panel, ncol = 2) +   labs(title = "Sales by Category Across Groups", x = "Category", y = "Sales") +   theme_minimal()  p p <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(x = wt, y = mpg)) +   geom_point(size = 2, color = "steelblue") +   facet_grid(vs ~ am,     labeller = labeller(       vs = c("0" = "V-engine", "1" = "Straight"),       am = c("0" = "Automatic", "1" = "Manual")     )   ) +   labs(     title = "MPG vs Weight by Engine Type and Transmission",     x = "Weight (1000 lbs)",     y = "Miles per Gallon"   ) +   theme_minimal()  p

title: Getting Started with MAIDR
text: MAIDR (Multimodal Access Interactive Data Representation) R package makes data visualizations accessible users visual impairments. converts ggplot2 Base R plots interactive, accessible formats : Keyboard navigation - Explore data using arrow keys Screen reader support - Full ARIA labels descriptions Sonification - Hear data patterns sound HTML/SVG output - Standalone accessible visualizations MAIDR helps data scientists researchers create inclusive visualizations everyone can explore, regardless visual ability.

title: Getting Started with MAIDR
text: Install development version GitHub:

title: Getting Started with MAIDR
text: MAIDR works two main functions: show() - Display interactive plot RStudio Viewer browser save_html() - Save plot standalone HTML file

title: Getting Started with MAIDR
code: library(maidr) library(ggplot2)  # Create sample data sales_data <- data.frame(   Product = c("A", "B", "C", "D"),   Sales = c(150, 230, 180, 290) )  # Create a bar chart p <- ggplot(sales_data, aes(x = Product, y = Sales)) +   geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "steelblue") +   labs(     title = "Product Sales by Category",     x = "Product",     y = "Sales Amount"   ) +   theme_minimal()  # Display interactively show(p)  # Or save as HTML file save_html(p, "sales_chart.html")

title: Getting Started with MAIDR
text: MAIDR also works Base R plotting functions:
code: library(maidr)  # Create a simple barplot categories <- c("A", "B", "C", "D") values <- c(150, 230, 180, 290)  barplot(   values,   names.arg = categories,   col = "steelblue",   main = "Product Sales by Category",   xlab = "Product",   ylab = "Sales Amount" )  # Note: For Base R plots, call show() with NO arguments # after creating the plot show()

title: Getting Started with MAIDR
text: default, MAIDR auto-detects internet availability loads MAIDR.js library CDN online. can control behavior use_cdn parameter: parameter works save_html(): use use_cdn = FALSE: - Creating portable HTML files offline viewing - Sharing files users may internet access - Ensuring reproducibility specific MAIDR.js version
code: library(maidr) library(ggplot2)  p <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(x = factor(cyl), y = mpg)) +   geom_bar(stat = "identity")  # Auto-detect (default) - uses CDN if internet available show(p)  # Force CDN (requires internet when viewing) show(p, use_cdn = TRUE)  # Force bundled/local files (works offline) show(p, use_cdn = FALSE) # Save with CDN links (smaller file, needs internet to view) save_html(p, "plot_cdn.html", use_cdn = TRUE)  # Save with bundled files (larger file, works offline) save_html(p, "plot_offline.html", use_cdn = FALSE)

title: Using MAIDR in Shiny Applications
text: MAIDR integrates seamlessly Shiny applications, allowing create accessible, interactive data visualizations web apps. vignette shows use MAIDR’s Shiny functions build accessible dashboards.

title: Using MAIDR in Shiny Applications
text: MAIDR provides two functions Shiny integration: maidr_output() - UI function create output container render_maidr() - Server function render plots functions work together display accessible MAIDR plots Shiny app.

title: Using MAIDR in Shiny Applications
text: ’s complete minimal Shiny app using MAIDR:
code: library(shiny) library(ggplot2) library(maidr)  # Define UI ui <- fluidPage(   titlePanel("MAIDR in Shiny - Basic Example"),   sidebarLayout(     sidebarPanel(       selectInput("plot_type",         "Choose Plot Type:",         choices = c("Bar Chart", "Histogram", "Scatter")       ),       br(),       p("The plot on the right is fully accessible with keyboard navigation and screen reader support.")     ),     mainPanel(       h3("Interactive Accessible Plot"),       maidr_output("accessible_plot", height = "500px")     )   ) )  # Define Server server <- function(input, output) {   output$accessible_plot <- render_maidr({     if (input$plot_type == "Bar Chart") {       # Bar chart       bar_data <- data.frame(         Category = c("A", "B", "C", "D"),         Value = c(25, 40, 30, 35)       )       ggplot(bar_data, aes(x = Category, y = Value)) +         geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "steelblue") +         labs(title = "Sample Bar Chart") +         theme_minimal()     } else if (input$plot_type == "Histogram") {       # Histogram       hist_data <- data.frame(values = rnorm(500, mean = 100, sd = 15))       ggplot(hist_data, aes(x = values)) +         geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "coral", color = "black") +         labs(title = "Sample Histogram", x = "Values", y = "Frequency") +         theme_minimal()     } else {       # Scatter plot       scatter_data <- data.frame(         x = rnorm(50),         y = rnorm(50),         group = sample(c("Group A", "Group B"), 50, replace = TRUE)       )       ggplot(scatter_data, aes(x = x, y = y, color = group)) +         geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.7) +         labs(title = "Sample Scatter Plot") +         theme_minimal()     }   }) }  # Run the app shinyApp(ui = ui, server = server)

title: Using MAIDR in Shiny Applications
text: ’s comprehensive dashboard multiple plots:
code: library(shiny) library(shinydashboard) library(ggplot2) library(maidr)  # Define UI ui <- dashboardPage(   dashboardHeader(title = "MAIDR Dashboard"),   dashboardSidebar(     sidebarMenu(       menuItem("Bar Charts", tabName = "bar", icon = icon("chart-bar")),       menuItem("Statistical Plots", tabName = "stats", icon = icon("chart-line")),       menuItem("Scatter Plots", tabName = "scatter", icon = icon("circle"))     )   ),   dashboardBody(     tabItems(       # Bar charts tab       tabItem(         tabName = "bar",         fluidRow(           box(             title = "Product Sales",             status = "primary",             solidHeader = TRUE,             width = 6,             maidr_output("bar_plot", height = "400px")           ),           box(             title = "Regional Comparison",             status = "primary",             solidHeader = TRUE,             width = 6,             maidr_output("dodged_bar_plot", height = "400px")           )         )       ),        # Statistical plots tab       tabItem(         tabName = "stats",         fluidRow(           box(             title = "Distribution",             status = "primary",             solidHeader = TRUE,             width = 6,             maidr_output("histogram_plot", height = "400px")           ),           box(             title = "Box Plot Comparison",             status = "primary",             solidHeader = TRUE,             width = 6,             maidr_output("boxplot_plot", height = "400px")           )         )       ),        # Scatter plots tab       tabItem(         tabName = "scatter",         fluidRow(           box(             title = "Correlation Analysis",             status = "primary",             solidHeader = TRUE,             width = 12,             maidr_output("scatter_plot", height = "500px")           )         )       )     )   ) )  # Define Server server <- function(input, output) {   # Bar plot   output$bar_plot <- render_maidr({     sales_data <- data.frame(       Product = c("A", "B", "C", "D", "E"),       Sales = c(150, 230, 180, 290, 210)     )      ggplot(sales_data, aes(x = Product, y = Sales)) +       geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "steelblue") +       labs(title = "Product Sales", x = "Product", y = "Sales Amount") +       theme_minimal()   })    # Dodged bar plot   output$dodged_bar_plot <- render_maidr({     regional_data <- data.frame(       Region = rep(c("North", "South", "East"), each = 2),       Quarter = rep(c("Q1", "Q2"), 3),       Sales = c(100, 120, 150, 180, 90, 110)     )      ggplot(regional_data, aes(x = Region, y = Sales, fill = Quarter)) +       geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.8)) +       labs(title = "Regional Sales Comparison") +       theme_minimal()   })    # Histogram   output$histogram_plot <- render_maidr({     hist_data <- data.frame(values = rnorm(1000, mean = 100, sd = 15))      ggplot(hist_data, aes(x = values)) +       geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "coral", color = "black") +       labs(title = "Score Distribution", x = "Score", y = "Frequency") +       theme_minimal()   })    # Boxplot   output$boxplot_plot <- render_maidr({     ggplot(iris, aes(x = Species, y = Petal.Length)) +       geom_boxplot(fill = "lightblue", alpha = 0.7) +       labs(title = "Petal Length by Species") +       theme_minimal()   })    # Scatter plot   output$scatter_plot <- render_maidr({     scatter_data <- data.frame(       height = rnorm(100, 170, 10),       weight = rnorm(100, 70, 8),       gender = sample(c("Male", "Female"), 100, replace = TRUE)     )      ggplot(scatter_data, aes(x = height, y = weight, color = gender)) +       geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.7) +       labs(         title = "Height vs Weight Analysis",         x = "Height (cm)",         y = "Weight (kg)"       ) +       theme_minimal()   }) }  # Run the app shinyApp(ui = ui, server = server)

title: Using MAIDR in Shiny Applications
text: MAIDR works seamlessly Shiny’s reactive programming:
code: library(shiny) library(ggplot2) library(maidr)  ui <- fluidPage(   titlePanel("Reactive MAIDR Plot"),   sidebarLayout(     sidebarPanel(       sliderInput("n_points",         "Number of Points:",         min = 10,         max = 200,         value = 50       ),       selectInput("distribution",         "Distribution:",         choices = c("Normal", "Uniform", "Exponential")       )     ),     mainPanel(       maidr_output("reactive_plot", height = "500px")     )   ) )  server <- function(input, output) {   # Reactive data generation   plot_data <- reactive({     set.seed(123) # For reproducibility      if (input$distribution == "Normal") {       data.frame(x = rnorm(input$n_points))     } else if (input$distribution == "Uniform") {       data.frame(x = runif(input$n_points, -3, 3))     } else {       data.frame(x = rexp(input$n_points, rate = 1) - 1)     }   })    # Render plot   output$reactive_plot <- render_maidr({     ggplot(plot_data(), aes(x = x)) +       geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "skyblue", color = "black") +       labs(         title = paste(input$distribution, "Distribution"),         subtitle = paste("n =", input$n_points),         x = "Value",         y = "Frequency"       ) +       theme_minimal()   }) }  shinyApp(ui = ui, server = server)

title: maidr - Accessible ggplot2 & R Plots with Sonification
text: maidr (Multimodal Access Interactive Data Representation) makes data visualizations accessible users visual impairments. converts ggplot2 Base R plots interactive, accessible HTML/SVG formats keyboard navigation, screen reader support, sonification. package provides two main functions: show() displays interactive accessible plot RStudio Viewer browser save_html() exports plot standalone HTML file

what: maidr: Multimodal Access and Interactive Data Representation — maidr-package
title: maidr: Multimodal Access and Interactive Data Representation — maidr-package
text: 'maidr' package provides accessible, interactive visualizations MAIDR (Multimodal Access Interactive Data Representation) system. converts 'ggplot2' Base R plots accessible HTML/SVG formats keyboard navigation, screen reader support, sonification capabilities. enables users visual impairments independently explore understand data visualizations multiple sensory modalities.

title: maidr: Multimodal Access and Interactive Data Representation — maidr-package
text: show: Display interactive MAIDR plot browser RStudio Viewer save_html: Save plot standalone HTML file run_example: Run interactive example plots maidr_on: Enable automatic MAIDR interception RMarkdown maidr_off: Disable automatic MAIDR interception render_maidr: Render MAIDR plots Shiny applications maidr_output: Create MAIDR output container Shiny UI

title: maidr: Multimodal Access and Interactive Data Representation — maidr-package
text: package supports wide variety plot types 'ggplot2' Base R plotting systems: ggplot2 plots: Bar charts (simple, grouped, stacked) - geom_bar(), geom_col() Histograms - geom_histogram() Scatter plots - geom_point() Line plots - geom_line() Box plots - geom_boxplot() Violin plots - geom_violin() Heat maps - geom_tile() Density/smooth curves - geom_density(), geom_smooth() Faceted plots - facet_wrap(), facet_grid() Multi-panel layouts (via 'patchwork' package) Multi-layered plot combinations Base R plots: Bar plots (simple, grouped, stacked) - barplot() Histograms - hist() Scatter line plots - plot(), points(), lines() Box plots - boxplot() Heat maps - image(), heatmap() Multi-panel layouts - par(mfrow), par(mfcol)

title: maidr: Multimodal Access and Interactive Data Representation — maidr-package
text: Keyboard Navigation: Use arrow keys explore data points Screen Reader Support: ARIA labels live regions announcements Sonification: Audio representation data patterns Text Summaries: Automatic statistical descriptions Grid Navigation: Efficient exploration scatter plots

title: maidr: Multimodal Access and Interactive Data Representation — maidr-package
text: package integrates seamlessly : RStudio: Direct display Viewer pane RMarkdown/Quarto: Automatic rendering maidr_on() Shiny: Interactive plots Shiny apps via render_maidr() Standalone HTML: Export plots sharing save_html()

title: maidr: Multimodal Access and Interactive Data Representation — maidr-package
text: create first accessible plot: Base R plots:
code: library(maidr) library(ggplot2)  # Create a ggplot2 plot p <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(x = factor(cyl), y = mpg)) +   geom_boxplot()  # Display as interactive MAIDR plot show(p)  # Or save to HTML file save_html(p, file = "my_plot.html") library(maidr)  # Create a Base R plot barplot(table(mtcars$cyl))  # Display as interactive MAIDR plot show()

text: Package website: https://r.maidr.ai/ MAIDR project: https://maidr.ai/ GitHub repository: https://github.com/xability/r-maidr Get started: vignette("getting-started", package = "maidr") Shiny integration: vignette("shiny-integration", package = "maidr")

code: # List all available examples run_example() #> Available MAIDR examples: #> ggplot2 examples: #>   - bar #>   - boxplot #>   - dodged_bar #>   - faceted #>   - heatmap #>   - histogram #>   - line #>   - multiline #>   - patchwork #>   - scatter #>   - smooth #>   - stacked_bar #>   - violin #>  #> base_r examples: #>   - bar #>   - boxplot #>   - dodged_bar #>   - faceted_point #>   - heatmap #>   - histogram #>   - line #>   - multiline #>   - scatter #>   - smooth #>   - stacked_bar #>  #> Usage: #>   run_example("bar")                 # Run ggplot2 bar chart #>   run_example("histogram", "base_r") # Run Base R histogram  if (interactive()) {   # Run ggplot2 bar chart example   run_example("bar")    # Run Base R histogram example   run_example("histogram", type = "base_r") }

maidr 0.2.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-03-07

title: maidr 0.2.0
text: Added violin plot support ‘ggplot2’ (geom_violin()), including vertical horizontal orientations. Violin plots produce two interactive layers: box-summary layer (violin_box) min, Q1, median, Q3, max highlights, KDE density-curve layer (violin_kde) navigable density points. Added Ramer-Douglas-Peucker (RDP) curve simplification reduce KDE density points ~30 per violin preserving shape fidelity. SVG coordinate injection violin KDE points enables accurate highlight positioning maidr frontend.

title: maidr 0.2.0
text: Renamed option maidr.enabled maidr.auto_show clarity. Added domMapping.iqrDirection support violin box layers, aligning existing box plot pattern correct Q1/Q3 highlighting gridSVG Y-flip transforms. Added plot augmentation API (augment_plot(), needs_augmentation()) LayerProcessor base class, enabling processors inject additional geom layers rendering. Added multi-layer expansion orchestrator plot types produce one maidr layer single geom.

title: maidr 0.2.0
text: Added violin plot examples show(), save_html(), vignettes, example scripts. Updated DESCRIPTION list violin plots supported type.

title: maidr 0.1.1
text: Resubmission CRAN archival. Fixes CRAN policy compliance issues.

title: maidr 0.1.1
text: Removed assign(..., envir = .GlobalEnv) calls violated CRAN policy. Base R function wrappers now installed package namespace .onLoad controlled via active/inactive flag, eliminating modification user’s global environment. Removed attach() usage produced R CMD check NOTE. Fixed Rd documentation warning caused unicode escape sequences prefix_to_currency_code parameter documentation.
